issue.body:
Once the glyph-size data exists (see the sidenote tuning and projection issues), `expand_row_blocks` column detection should become sidenote-aware: the geometric column splitter shouldn't break on a sidenote that produces a narrow third column.

Currently rare in the PGDP corpus, so this is a follow-up to the glyph-size work, not standalone. Do not pick this before the sidenote tuning slice has shipped.

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (Multi-column body detection enhancements).
Spec: docs/specs/03-reorganize-pipeline.md

## Acceptance
- [ ] `expand_row_blocks` consumes sidenote tags from layout/glyph-size data
- [ ] Fixture: a page with body + sidenote does NOT split into a spurious third column
- [ ] Fixture: a real two-column page still splits correctly
- [ ] No regressions across the layout-regression fixture suite

nodes.body: Triage decision: approved + needs-spec. Spec child issue: #49. Run `/spec-from-issue 49` to produce the design spec.

issue.title: Multi-column body detection: make expand_row_blocks sidenote-aware

2. `/decompose-spec docs/specs/NN-<slug>.md` — creates per-spec milestone + child issues with `bot:ship-issue-ready` where appropriate.

## Triage decision

Approved as needing-spec during the 2026-05-11 backlog triage sweep — this feature requires design work before implementation; not a one-PR tracking-style chore.

Inherits effort/model labels from the parent feature-request.

issue.body:
Filed from the pd-book-tools deep code/security review.

Report: `docs/research/2026-05-22-deep-code-security-review.md`
Finding: #6

Moved from mistakenly filed meta issue: ConcaveTrillion/ocr-container-meta#299

Evidence:

- `pd_book_tools/geometry/image_ops.py:58`
- `pd_book_tools/geometry/image_ops.py:59`
- `pd_book_tools/geometry/image_ops.py:102`

Pixel-space `BoundingBox` supports float coordinates, and `refine(..., expand_beyond_original=True)` can produce them. `_extract_roi()` and `_connected_content_bbox_from_image_thresh()` use those floats directly as NumPy slice bounds.

Impact:

- Valid boxes can raise `TypeError: slice indices must be integers...` in `refine_bbox()`, `crop_top_bbox()`, `crop_bottom_bbox()`, and connected-component expansion.

Remediation:

- Convert pixel coordinates to integer ROI bounds with a documented rounding policy.
- Clamp bounds before slicing.
- Add tests for float pixel boxes and refine-then-crop flows.

---
This issue was filed from a read-only review. Confirm behavior with a focused failing test before implementation where practical.
issue.title: pd-book-tools: valid pixel-space float boxes crash bbox image ops
